Just now

The Biggest Open AI Model Ever Just Beat Claude at Coding. It Also Hallucinates Half the Time.Kimi K3 is 2.8 trillion parameters, open-weight, and ranked #1 at frontend code — and its hallucination rate just jumped to 51%. I fact-checked every major claim. Both halves of this story are true, and the tension between them is the most honest picture of AI in 2026 you’ll get.
Two things happened this month that don’t sound like they belong in the same story.
A Chinese lab most people hadn’t heard of a year ago released the largest open-weight AI model in history — and in blind developer testing, it beat Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol at frontend coding. Then the independent evaluators finished their runs and published a second number: the same model fabricates answers at a 51% hallucination rate — worse than its own predecessor.
Champion coder. Confident liar. Same model, same week.
If you only read the hype threads, you got the first half. If you only read the skeptics, you got the second. This post is the whole thing: what Kimi K3 actually is, every major claim fact-checked against primary sources, why “open weights” doesn’t mean what most people think, and what a builder should actually do about it. (The story broke widely via Fireship’s excellent five-minute report — embedded below if you want the…
